channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Former Dem fundraiser sees 'overwhelmingly positive' response as her longtime donors give to Trump inaugural
item.description: Former Democrat fundraiser Lindy Li, named to President-elect Trump’s inaugural fundraising committee, says many of her longtime donors have given enthusiastically.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First hearing in 'Trump-proof' California special session canceled as chairman's district hit by wildfires
item.description: A California Assembly committee chairman canceled the planned first hearing in a special "Trump-proof" session due to wildfires ravaging his legislative district.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Border leaders say their communities are regaining ‘normalcy’ ahead of Trump inauguration
item.description: Leaders at the southern border tell Fox News Digital that illegal immigrant numbers are down, and their communities are regaining a sense of “normalcy" ahead of President-elect Trump’s inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cap-and-trade returns: NY plans to force big oil to ‘invest’ in ‘green’ by paying for emissions
item.description: In her State of the State address, New York Gov. Kathy Hochul is expected to propose a cap-and-invest program for energy producers that emit pollution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This is what the Homeland Security secretary has to say about his border record
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the waning days of the Biden administration, Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas defended his agency's work to tamp down border-crossing numbers and argued against breaking apart the sprawling department in a wide-ranging interview with The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court turns back Utah's push to wrest control of public land from the federal government
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday turned back a push by the state of Utah to wrest control of vast areas of public land from the federal government, marking a small victory for land conservation advocates who worry that similar efforts may escalate in a Republican-controlled Washington.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth was 'incredibly talented, battle-proven leader,' military evaluations show
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's defense secretary nominee Pete Hegseth was praised in military evaluations as an "incredibly talented, battle-proven leader.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas Supreme Court to decide legal battle over El Paso migrant shelter’s future
item.description: Justices on Monday heard arguments from Annunciation House, which is fighting to stay open, and the Texas Attorney General’s office, which claims the shelter’s work with migrants violates state law.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Alejandro Serrano, KPRC 2

item.title: Cannon allows for release of Smith report on Trump election interference
item.description: U.S. District Judge Aileen Cannon on Monday allowed the release of the volume of special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with Donald Trump’s efforts to block the peaceful transfer of power after the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Carrie Underwood, Village People to perform at Trump's inauguration events
item.description: Country music star Carrie Underwood will perform “America the Beautiful” at Donald Trump’s inauguration next week and the 1970s hitmakers Village People will perform at two inaugural events.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Liberal group threatens to sue law enforcement in blue state for helping ICE
item.description: A liberal activist group warns sheriffs in Colorado that they will hold them personally liable if they cooperate with ICE officials, including working to deport criminal illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns back challenge to strict gun licensing law in Maryland
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Monday turned back a challenge to a strict gun licensing law in Maryland. The high court declined to hear the case in a brief order handed down without elaboration, as is typical.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis's call for special session 'premature,' Senate President, House Speaker say
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a special legislative session to address illegal immigration, among other issues, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth could lead troops who'd face getting fired for actions he's done in the past
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — If Pete Hegseth were still in uniform, his extramarital affairs and a decision to flatly ignore a combat commander's directive would not just be drawing the attention of senators — they could have run afoul of military law.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Legislature 101: How bills become laws
item.description: Here’s a rundown of how legislation moves through the Capitol, the ways lawmakers operate and who the power players are.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By María Méndez, KPRC 2

item.title: A tough-on-crime approach is back in US state capitols
item.description: Among his first actions in office, new Missouri Gov. Mike Kehoe issued a volley of orders targeting crime.
item.dc:creator: David A. Lieb, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden speaks with relatives of Americans held by Taliban, but deal to bring them home still elusive
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden spoke Sunday with relatives of three Americans the U.S. government is looking to bring home from Afghanistan, but no agreement has been reached on a deal to get them back, family members said.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Jack Smith shouldn't be allowed to release report
item.description: "Why would Deranged Jack Smith be allowed to issue a “report” on a complete and total Witch Hunt against me, strictly for political purposes,” Trump said in a Truth Social post.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Vance says Jan. 6 participants who committed violence 'obviously' shouldn't be pardoned
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance says people responsible for the violence during the Capitol riot “obviously” should not be pardoned, as President-elect Donald Trump is promising to use his clemency power on behalf of many of those who tried on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans urge Trump nominees: Don't take the bait from Democrats
item.description: Senate Republicans are urging President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees to resist taking the bait from Democrats when their high-stakes confirmation hearings kick off next week, marking a make-or-break moment for some of his choices.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Pence said he congratulated Trump on his win at Carter’s funeral
item.description: The Pences were seated a row behind Trump with the other vice presidents, while Trump sat next to former President Obama.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Congress braces for California wildfire fallout
item.description: House lawmakers are bracing for what could be a contentious battle over emergency spending in the wake of the wildfires that are tearing through Southern California — an unprecedented disaster that’s already estimated to have caused more than $50 billion in damage in and around Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU sues Gov. DeSantis on behalf of Florida voters over special elections
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union is suing Gov. Ron DeSantis for not calling special elections for state House and Senate seats.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: DHS extends TPS for Salvadorans, potentially complicating Trump's plans
item.description: The Biden administration on Friday extended a key humanitarian program for El Salvadoran nationals in a move that could complicate the incoming Trump administration’s plans to do away with long-term immigrant protections.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, NewsNation

item.title: President-elect Trump to be sentenced in hush money case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a singular moment in U.S. history, President-elect Donald Trump faces sentencing Friday for his New York hush money conviction after the nation's highest court refused to intervene.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Homeless mental health and affordability headline NYC mayor’s State of the City address
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Mayor Eric Adams on Thursday laid out an ambitious agenda for 2025 while fighting for his political life. His State of the City address was the last of his first term in office as he faces a tough reelection battle.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Nevada Democrats join Republicans in bill to detain migrants accused of certain crimes
item.description: All five members of Southern Nevada’s congressional delegation said they support a Republican proposal to detain immigrants in the country illegally who commit certain crimes.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams delivers 4th State of the City address
item.description: HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York City Mayor Eric Adams delivered his fourth State of the City address at The Apollo in Harlem on Thursday. Adams discussed topics like public safety, the cost of living, and new initiatives to build more in the city.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Justice Department says it plans to release only part of special counsel's Trump report for now
item.description: The Justice Department said Wednesday that it will release special counsel Jack Smith’s findings on Donald Trump’s efforts to undo the results of the 2020 presidential election but will keep under wraps for now the rest of the record focused on the president-elect's hoarding of classified documents
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President-elect Trump asks Supreme Court to block sentencing in hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Future of Florida's shores: Biden's offshore oil drilling ban faces political backlash
item.description: President Joe Biden issued a ban on offshore oil drilling in federal waters, including the Gulf of Mexico. But President-elect Donald Trump plans to reverse the move when he takes office in less than two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump: 'All hell will break out' if Hamas hostages not released by inauguration
item.description: "It will not be good for Hamas, and it will not be good, frankly, for anyone," Trump said during a press conference at his Mar-a-Lago residence. "All hell will break out. I don't have to say any more, but that's what it is.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Board of Supervisors splits votes on new chair following Vargas' exit
item.description: The San Diego County Board of Supervisors punted a vote on who will succeed Nora Vargas as chair after competing motions to elevate either Terra Lawson-Remer or Jim Desmond to the role failed in votes down partisan lines.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Trump refuses to rule out use of military force to take control of Greenland, Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday said he would not rule out the use of military force to seize control of the Panama Canal and Greenland, as he declared U.S. control of both to be vital to American national security.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ZEKE MILLER Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
